item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm also wondering this. The best I can think of is that the dreams it causes are not themselves containable, and thus an aspect of the entity is beyond Foundation control? That, and there is a great deal about it which remains wholly unexplained - where/when/ is the city, if it is real; what are the entities depicted within; what does its consumption and regurgitation profit it?

But, I mean, if its Keter because we don't know how it works… then almost everything is Keter. I'd argue that Euclid or Safe would fit better, since either: we are fairly certain we know what it wants, and can give it that (unstoppable dream affecting not withstanding) / it can not harm the things we give it, and we know what to give it.

I'd fall on the side of Euclid, if it were me - Safe being a bit optimistic, but Keter seems very strange. I know the common criticism of Keter entities is "It's not deadly/damaging/actively killing something right now, so why is it Keter?" - but I would ask how this is: uncontained/uncontainable/capable of vast destruction if unappeased/in a position of equal power to the Foundation. These are my usual hallmarks of Keter - under which does this fall?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Know I am dead posting a bit, but why is this keter? It seems like it will stay in its "box"? It does not pose any serious threat to civilization.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
♪ Sweet dreams are made of… Bobbit Worms? ♪

In all seriousness, I really dig the idea. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eaten by a giant worm and having awesome dreams?
Put me on the SCP-3282-1 list. +1

item.title: Re: And he's back!
item.content:encoded:
Working with AnOddlyClassyGecko has been pretty awesome.

Aw shucks :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it! Thanks for the comment. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of the Alaskan bull worm from Spongebob.

Well written and interesting. Very nice work! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the upvote, and thanks again for your review in the forums! :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like I said in the forum, I am happy to see the finished product. + 1

item.title: Re: And he's back!
item.content:encoded:
It's been quite the journey! Big, big thanks to everyone who reviewed this in the forums and the chat!

Working with AnOddlyClassyGecko has been pretty awesome. There was a lot of discussion and brainstorming that happened, and I hope that the result is an enjoyable read!

item.title: And he's back!
item.content:encoded:
Through building the broken are the disgraced renewed

See what I did there with the quote because this a revision of a bad article that I made and I'm 'building the broken'…. nevermind

And he's back with the worm again! I hope it turns out better this time. A HUGE thanks to Zyn for co-authoring this with me! I probably couldn't have gotten anywhere near this level of quality of both concept and execution without her help. Anyway, hope you enjoyed!

Credits:
Zyn (co-authoring)
Leveritas (Reviewing)
Toa_Vine (Reviewing)
Stormbreath (Reviewing)
CaptainKirby (Reviewing)

Oh yeah image is from here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Eunice_aphroditois.jpg and is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 2.0 Generic license.

EDIT: Fixed the typo in the title. That could've been really embarrassing if anybody pointed that out.

And no I will not tell you what it was.
